mentions.note: IPO'd today at $20, closed $19.01; outsourced mfg for space/defense (like Flex/Jabil/Celestica but military-focused). $1.06B backlog = 1.75x revenue; EBITDA margins low 20s. 31x 2026E multiple slightly elevated but story likely cheaper than it looks. Prefer mid-teens entry but okay to buy here—not chasing, valuation not crazy. Greenbrier retains 81%.

IPO outsource manufacturer for aerospace/defense; uniquely positioned at day-one-to-full-scale production. Supports both legacy primes and SpaceX/Blue Origin. Decision velocity (3–5yr vs. 10–20yr programs) and IP-enabled manufacturing enable fast response to urgent drone/missile/satellite demand post-Iran restocking. CEO Trip Ferguson projects urgency on generational geopolitical demand. Cramer: rare IPO that gets discovered over time with strong fundamentals; will outperform hype-cycle plays.
